Not when it demands attention, but when it earns it. 

@balenciaga #Balenciaga

posts.caption:
#ManifestCover
There is a quiet power to Rukmini Vasanth (@rukmini_vasanth). One that doesn’t seek attention, yet holds it effortlessly.
For Manifest’s May–June cover, she steps into an imaginative world where temple jewellery meets contemporary silhouettes, blurring the lines between memory and modernity. She embodies a woman in transition, grounded, luminous, and yet constantly becoming.
“I’m not very good at describing myself,” she says, almost in passing.
Yet, in the same breath, her choices reveal a quiet clarity of intent. “I don’t want to isolate my character. I need to understand the whole world it exists in.”
Between restraint and reinvention, stillness and movement, she exists in that rare in-between. Not fully defined, not confined. Just on the cusp of a wider sky.
Our latest issue is now live on Magzter. Read the full cover story from the link in bio.

Of all the celebrations that make up a BIG fat Indian wedding, the Mehfil night has a rhythm of its own. It’s slower, serene, poetic, spiritual, and has an old-world charm to it. Filled with Qawwali performances, the atmosphere feels equal parts romantic and reverent.
Whether you’re channeling Ranveer Singh’s maximalist energy or leaning into understated elegance with ivory, beige, and intricate embroidery, we’ve rounded up groom looks for your Mehfil that feels like poetry and magic.

posts.caption:
Fourteen years later, after navigating different cities, careers, milestones, and the countless versions of themselves that adulthood demands, a Marwari girl and an a Bengali boy found themselves back where it all began. Anupama Jethalia (@anupamajethalia) and Abhinav Saha (@full.of.saha), their love story began with the simple joys: canteen conversations, shared chocolates, endless cups of coffee, and two students trying to figure out life in Jaipur.
So when it came time to get married, Jaipur wasn’t a destination. It was a return. A quiet homecoming to the city that witnessed their first chapter, and the place where, fourteen years later, the story came full circle.”
Set against the timeless charm of Mundota Fort & Palace, their wedding unfolded as a celebration of everything that had shaped them over the last decade and a half. There was a female pundit leading the ceremony, a conscious choice that reflected their values.
Written: Kratagya Rathore @kratagya_rathore

Here’s a place that dips into regional homestyle recipes, served in a charming, old-world space.

posts.caption:
The book and fashion worlds have been flirting. At Climax, an East Village shop that sells rare books and ephemera, they’re making out.
Climax customers can flip through a Vivienne Westwood monograph, a collection of short stories by Mary Gaitskill or assorted erotica. They can also shop Climax’s playful merch, like their colorful T-shirts, hoodies and latex bags. But what do all of these offerings have in common?
“I think it’s a feeling,” said the owner Isabella Burley, whose idiosyncratic taste has attracted a fashionable clientele. Alexa Chung and Paloma Elsesser are regulars. Timothée Chalamet visited recently. And the store has hosted book signings for designers including Simone Rocha.
At the link in our bio, read more about how @climaxbooks is bringing the fashion and literary worlds closer together and see more photos from inside the store. Photos by @gab
posts.alt_text: Photo by New York Times Fashion & Style on June 09, 2026. May be a Twitter screenshot of one or more people and text that says 'ROc PUOFT Would You Bet on Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's Wedding?'.

posts.caption:
On sites like Kalshi and Polymarket, where there are markets for betting on everything from game scores to political appointments to daily high temperatures, millions of dollars in bets are being placed in a seemingly niche category: Taylor Swift’s wedding with Travis Kelce.
On Kalshi, users have bet over $2 million on markets related to the wedding — $1.49 million on the location alone, which is in the top 5 percent of markets on the site. And on Polymarket, the guest list is a hot topic.
The prediction market sites are changing the fandom experience. For some, it’s a convergence of the sleuthing Swiftie fans are known for and the intrigue around an ultraprivate event for which little has been publicly confirmed. For others, it’s an invasion of privacy.
Does betting on the star's personal life cross a line? At the link in our bio, read what fans and experts think about the phenomenon. Photo by Jason Miller/Getty Images
posts.alt_text: Photo by New York Times Fashion & Style on June 09, 2026. May be a Twitter screenshot of one or more people and text that says 'ROc PUOFT Would You Bet on Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's Wedding?'.

posts.caption:
After the Tony Awards on Sunday, a crowd that included Daniel Radcliffe, Cole Escola and Carrie Coon celebrated with whiskey ice cream at Rockefeller Center before heading to the Carlyle hotel on the Upper East Side for another after-party filled with Champagne and show tunes.
The gathering on the rink level at Rockefeller Center, the official after-party, was a brief but bustling stop for most. Attendees included industry veterans — the “Saturday Night Live” creator Lorne Michaels, a “Schmigadoon!” producer; and Kelli O’Hara, nominated for “Fallen Angels” — and many first-time Tony nominees.
By 1 a.m., many of the nominees had left for the Carlyle, where the theater publicist Rick Miramontez and the producers John Gore and Jamie DuMont were hosting their annual late-night bash for several hundred guests. Among the crowd were Megan Thee Stallion, Nicole Scherzinger and Tom Felton, who is currently reprising his role as Draco Malfoy in the "Harry Potter" films in “Harry Potter and the Cursed Child” on Broadway.
Our reporters @smbahr14 and @nancylcoleman chatted with the partygoers as they celebrated theater's biggest night. Tap the link in our bio to get an inside look at the Tonys after-parties. Photos by @vnina and @poupayphoto
posts.alt_text: Photo shared by New York Times Fashion & Style on June 09, 2026 tagging @nytbooks, and @nyt_nyc. May be an image of poster, book, magazine, newsstand and text that says 'Her Bookstore Knows Sex Sells HLTICS พ3, CMA6KK 馬他味'.

posts.caption:
Among the major entertainment award shows, the Tony Awards are not always associated with the flashiest red (or, in this case, blue) carpets.  But that’s changing.
In the last few years, a number of film and television stars have made their Broadway debuts, bringing with them a slew of new theater fans, as well as the high-fashion labels that dress them. Maya Rudolph, who is currently starring as the titular character in “Oh, Mary!” wore Chanel, as did Aubrey Plaza, who walked the carpet with her partner, Christopher Abbott.
The magic of Tony Awards style often has to do with the eccentric ensembles theater makers don to celebrate their peers. This year, we saw fringed headdresses; cropped suit jackets that showed off abs; and a colorful mix of patterns.
Some of the most memorable looks of the night came from stars who took bold risks and the independent labels that angled for a moment in the spotlight. Tap the link in our bio to see the 10 unforgettable looks from the Tonys red carpet. Photos by @poupayphoto, Dimitrios Kambouris/Getty Images

posts.caption:
Some of the biggest stars of Broadway showed out at Radio City Music Hall on Sunday for the 79th Tony Awards.
The event doesn’t typically have the same density of high-end designer dresses and haute couture as the Academy Awards or the Met Gala. But these are showbiz folk, and it would be a mistake to expect anything less than spectacle from the people who reimagined “Cats” as a voguing ball and gave Frank-N-Furter a chain saw.
Daniel Radcliffe, nominated for best leading actor in a play for “Every Brilliant Thing,” Carrie Coon, a nominee for best leading actress in a play for “Bug,” and Bowen Yang, a producer of “Titaníque,” which is nominated for best musical, were among the stars to walk the midnight blue carpet. At the link in our bio, see what all of the nominees, presenters and invitees wore to make their entrances to the Tonys.
Photos by @poupayphoto, Evan Agostini/Invision, via @apnews and Kena Betancur/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
posts.alt_text: Photo shared by New York Times Fashion & Style on June 09, 2026 tagging @nytbooks, and @nyt_nyc. May be an image of poster, book, magazine, newsstand and text that says 'Her Bookstore Knows Sex Sells HLTICS พ3, CMA6KK 馬他味'.

posts.caption:
Among the Broadway stars making their entrances to the 79th Tony Awards are the cast and crew of “Cats: The Jellicle Ball.” André De Shields, nominated for best featured actor, Qween Jean, the show's costume designer and Zhailon Levingston, nominated for best direction of a musical have arrived to the show.
In the early prizes, Qween Jean won for best costume design of a musical, becoming the first openly transgender person to win a Tony Award, according to a “Cats” publicist. “We are here for the legacy of queer people, trans people,” Qween Jean said in her acceptance speech, adding, “We have to take up space, we have to shift the paradigm.”
At the link in our bio, see what all of the theaters biggest stars wore to the Tony Awards and read live updates fromt the show. Photos by Jenny Anderson/Getty Images, Dimitrios Kambouris/Getty Images

posts.caption:
This has to be one of my favourite editorial shoots I've seen this year, and that's probably because it was shot by #SzilveszterMako, whose images feel closer to theatre and painting than traditional fashion photography.
His editorials often have a dreamlike quality because of their painterly approach. Through dramatic lighting, strong contrasts between light and shadow he gives his subjects an almost sculptural presence. The clothing blends seamlessly into the composition, contributing to the overall mood and becoming an extension of the subject rather than something just being worn.
#Rihanna shot by @szilvesztermako for @ee72, styled by @edward_enninful wearing #Schiaparelli #Chanel #Dior Alexander McQueen & Bottega Veneta
posts.alt_text: Photo by umar on May 28, 2026. May be an image of purse and text that says 'ሀቢት What Is the Craziest Bag Chanel Has Ever Made?'.

posts.caption:
If you have any of these bags, please step forward… #KarlLagerfeld was known for his larger-than-life shows, and the spectacle didn’t just stop at the sets, but translated into his absurd yet highly collectible bags.
Think of the iconic hula hoop bag from Spring 2013, which Karl joked was designed for the beach so you could stick it into the sand and hang your towel from it (which fyi someone is currently selling on eBay for £145K.) Then there was the supermarket show, complete with milk carton bags and the #Chanel chain basket, or the Spring 2019 flap bag filled with sand.
The bags became souvenirs from those fantasy worlds and now #MatthieuBlazy is continuing that tradition, turning handbags into collectible objects that blur the line between fashion and art.
Which one is your favourite?
posts.alt_text: Photo shared by umar on June 08, 2026 tagging @badgalriri, @edward_enninful, and @ee72. May be an image of one or more people, makeup, magazine, costume, poster, headdress, dress and text that says 'This May Be Rihanna's Most Beautiful Shoot Yet'.

posts.caption:
#PierpaoloPiccoli use of craftsmanship and creativity is extraordinary, and one of my favourite examples was #Valentino Fall 2023 #HauteCouture. At first glance, the look appeared deceptively simple: a white shirt and a pair of jeans. However, a closer look revealed that the “jeans” were entirely hand-embroidered from thousands of beads.
Valentino’s artisans spent four weeks creating a trompe-l’œil illusion of denim. Using a digital 3D scan of real jeans as a guide, they embroidered beads diagonally to replicate denim’s twill weave and used approximately 80 different shades of microbeads to recreate the fading and texture of denim.
Transforming one of the most ordinary garments in fashion which we all wear, into a couture masterpiece.

posts.caption:
I can’t stop thinking about #PeggyGou’s insane bag  collection…which one is your favourite? (It’s the #Fendi onigiri bag for me)
#Hermes #Chanel #BottegaVeneta
posts.alt_text: Photo shared by umar on June 08, 2026 tagging @badgalriri, @edward_enninful, and @ee72. May be an image of one or more people, makeup, magazine, costume, poster, headdress, dress and text that says 'This May Be Rihanna's Most Beautiful Shoot Yet'.

posts.caption:
A large part of #DanielRoseberry’s success at #Schiaparelli is, rather than directly reproducing Elsa’s work, he uses her visual language but transformed into something much more contemporary. Many heritage brands get trapped by their archives, endlessly reproducing old signatures, while Daniel mines the archive for concepts while pushing it into a new century.
#ElsaSchiaparelli shocked people because nobody had seen her ideas before, so the most faithful tribute for Daniel is to keep inventing and create things that continue to make people stop, laugh, wonder (or argue) just like Elsa did.
I belive Daniel’s Schiaparelli is one of the rare luxury houses that still feels genuinely artistic. The collections don’t look driven primarily by trends, merchandising, or social-media algorithms. They often feel like couture-as-imagination, which is very close to Elsa’s original vision.
What do you think of Daniel’s @schiaparelli?
posts.alt_text: Photo shared by umar on June 08, 2026 tagging @badgalriri, @edward_enninful, and @ee72. May be an image of one or more people, makeup, magazine, costume, poster, headdress, dress and text that says 'This May Be Rihanna's Most Beautiful Shoot Yet'.

posts.caption:
@balenciaga’s new #ManoloBlahnik collaboration makes perfect sense for #PierpaoloPiccioli’s evolving vision for the house. It shows where #Balenciaga is headed after years defined by irony, provocation, and streetwear. Signalling to a sense of couture and refinement, we haven’t really seen in a while.
By collaborating with one of the most revered shoe designers, I love how Piccioli references the shared Spanish heritage of #CristóbalBalenciaga and @manoloblahnik which makes the partnership feel rooted in something deeper than celeb hype or marketing.
While theres still exaggerated sneakers for the fanbase established under #Demna, a pair of hand made crystal satin slingbacks seems to be the perfect welcome back for the customer searching for glamour and femininity once again.
What do you think of the collab?

posts.caption:
Veena Murthy Vijay celebrates 50 years in dance this year. That's 50 years of preserving and sharing a rich tradition, 50 years of tremendous discipline and hard work, and 50 years of storytelling through the language of dance. A performer, choreographer, teacher, and cultural advocate, she has dedicated her life to dance as an artistic expression and a medium for social awareness.
In an interview with SheThePeople, the Bengaluru-based Kuchipudi doyen reflects on this five-decade legacy and what has kept her going all these years.
"I started dancing when I was five. I trained in Bharatanatyam first, then Kathak, and by my early teens, I had begun learning Kuchipudi. It soon became my main dance form," recounted Veena. She credits much of her artistic journey to her father, a tax consultant and passionate patron of the arts.
When she first started in Kuchipudi, the dance form was still relatively unknown in Karnataka. Veena played a key role in introducing it to new audiences. "I can fully say that I was one of the first people to introduce Kuchipudi as a maargam (path) here, as a complete repertoire."
Visit www.shethepeople.tv to read full interview by Tanya Savkoor.

posts.caption:
There are over 150 million Indian women in menopause in 2026. Yet, they are completely invisible from the National Family Health Survey 6. Women between 40 and 59 years of age are not mentioned once in the report.
The quadrennial survey by the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare is a nationally representative data on health, family welfare, and demographic indicators. It tracks important information such as maternal health, child nutrition, immunisation, and family planning.
However, a large and critical demographic of women in India are missing from the latest report. This is just another example of how women in midlife continue to be systemically ignored and unsupported in social and health spheres.
Visit www.shethepeople.tv for more.

posts.caption:
Fashion styling is a job. Not a hobby. Not a passion project. Not something people do on the side because they love clothes.
It is a profession that takes years to build. The eye. The contacts. The knowledge of how the industry actually works across every sector, every brief, every type of client. That does not come from loving fashion. It comes from years of showing up, doing the work and figuring it out mostly alone.
And yet it is still one of the most undervalued roles in the creative industry.
Stylists are asked to work for free. For credit. For exposure. For a rate that does not come close to reflecting the skill, the hours or the experience involved. And somewhere along the way the industry decided that was normal. That stylists should be grateful for the opportunity. That loving what you do means you should not expect to be paid properly for it.
We are past that.
Behind every iconic image, every red carpet moment, every campaign that stopped you mid-scroll, there was a stylist. Building the looks, interpreting the brief, managing the pressure, absorbing the chaos so nobody else had to. Often without a credit. Sometimes without a proper rate.
That changes when stylists know their worth, charge it without apology and stop accepting the jobs that treat their career like a favour.
➡️➡️ If you are a working fashion stylist who is ready to do exactly that, comment STUDIO and we will send you the free trial link to The Studio of Stylist Elixir. Rate guides. Contracts. Portfolio tools. Masterclasses every Monday. Everything you need to build a career that reflects what you are actually worth.

posts.caption:
Be honest. How many times have you had to explain that your job is not just “picking out nice clothes.” 🙄
You smile and nod at the dinner party while someone tells you they could do what you do. Meanwhile you are the one who got the call at 11pm because the samples did not clear customs. 📦 You are the one who held the whole shoot together when the brief changed on the day. You are the one who went home, lay awake doing sums, and wondered why a job this demanding still feels this uncertain. 😮‍💨
That is the part nobody at the dinner party sees.
They think it is a glamorous day out with beautiful clothes and a coffee. ☕️ They do not see the fifty pulls from twelve showrooms. The personalities you manage on every set. The private client who needs handling with care, the celebrity look that has to be signed off in days, the commercial brief with notes at every stage. They do not see you building a look out of nothing while a whole crew waits on you, then keeping going through the quiet months when the diary goes still and you start to doubt yourself. 🥀
You are not bad at this. You are doing one of the hardest creative jobs there is, mostly on your own, with no one in your corner who actually gets it. 💔

posts.caption:
India entered the decade with a roadmap for managing its stray-dog population. The National Action Plan for Rabies Elimination, launched in 2021, aligned the country with the global goal of ending dog-mediated human rabies deaths by 2030. Revised Animal Birth Control Rules followed in 2023, reaffirming sterilisation and vaccination as the foundation of population management.
Then came 2025. Amid growing concern over dog attacks, the Supreme Court briefly backed the relocation of stray dogs in Delhi-NCR before returning to a framework centred on sterilisation and immunisation. Months later, it directed authorities to remove dogs from schools, hospitals, railway stations and bus stands. In 2026, the court permitted euthanasia for rabid, incurably ill and demonstrably dangerous dogs.
Yet every turn in the debate points back to the same question: what happens when court orders move faster than municipal capacity? Until vaccination, sterilisation and waste-management systems work at scale, each new attack risks restarting the cycle.
posts.alt_text: Photo by The Established on June 09, 2026. May be pop art of one or more people, poster and text that says 'HEALTH & WELLNESS BEAUTY IS SHIFTING FROM WHAT YOU APPLY TO WHAT YOU INGEST'.

posts.caption:
Indian aesthetics travel well online. Crafts, streets, colours and traditions are photographed, saved, referenced and repackaged. The attention can be flattering, until the people, histories and labour behind the image begin to slip out of frame.
This shift often begins with a particular way of looking. The urban gaze rarely arrives as contempt. It arrives as appreciation: the craftsperson’s hand, the old doorway, the bazaar in golden light, the dye-stained palm. Visibility can bring recognition, money and authorship. It can also flatten context into content.
When India becomes a moodboard, the question is not whether the world is looking, but what gets edited out.
Read more at the link in bio.
By Deepti Dadlani (@vitamindeeforall)
#IndianAesthetics #UrbanGaze #IndianCraft #DigitalCulture #CulturalCritique

posts.caption:
Lavender marriage in India is a cultural x-ray.
It reveals how marriage remains one of the country’s most powerful systems of legitimacy, holding together family honour, caste, class, kinship and respectability.
For many queer Indians, it can become a survival strategy. An arrangement that satisfies social expectation while protecting private truths. These marriages are not always about romance. They are about negotiating belonging without disappearing inside it.
They show how tradition adapts, how secrecy becomes strategy and how intimacy can be redrawn under pressure.
Lavender marriage forces a harder question: what does marriage in India really protect?
Read more at the link in bio.
By Tejaswi Subramanian (@tejnesss)
#lavendermarriage #queerindia #marriageofconvenience #indiansociety #identity

posts.caption:
For decades, it lived in kitchens across the south of India as a daily ritual. Slow decoction. Hot milk. Chicory-rich flavour. Steel tumblers. No moodboard required.
Now, filter kaapi sits on boutique café menus, in design-forward packaging and across slow-living reels. The drink has not changed as much as the gaze around it has.
The real story is not just flavour or nostalgia. It is a Starbucks-raised generation returning to a grandmother’s decoction and recognising it as culture, craft and taste memory.
Read more at the link in bio.
By Karishma Kuenzang (@kkuenzang)
#SouthIndianFilterCoffee #FilterKaapi #IndianCoffeeCulture #PremiumCoffee

So we spoke with founders Mia Heckmann, Madison Heckmann, and Olivia Halton about building with intention in a space that moves fast and forgets even faster. And before you ask: yes, I’ve tried it. It works.

posts.caption:
@badgalriri for @ee72 looks so good I’m genuinely losing my mind. This marks a cornerstone launch for EE72, the new global publication founded by @edward_enninful, who also steps in as creative director and stylist for the issue.
Shot by @szilvesztermako, the imagery feels almost unreal in texture: painted backdrops, candlelit tones, and a kind of classical stillness that makes everything feel centuries old in the best way.
Rihanna appears across multiple collector’s covers, moving between Dior Haute Couture, Schiaparelli, Chanel, and Givenchy. And of course—Fenty everywhere. Hair by Yusef Williams, makeup by Ammy Drammeh, nails by Jenny Longworth. They really pulled out all the stops.

posts.caption:
This past week in fashion was all about summer… at least on the surface. Summer Fridays hosted a creator trip in Saint-Tropez for their latest launch, Monday Swimwear gave everyone Swim Week FOMO, and Hermès staged a beautiful resort show in Los Angeles.
Elsewhere, it was more about strategic alignment.
Ladurée and Casablanca announced a summer collaboration, SkinCeuticals became the official skincare partner of Ferrari just ahead of the Monaco Grand Prix, and Phia secured a $35.5M Series A at a $185.5M valuation. Different priorities, same industry.
Stay tuned.

posts.caption:
Dua Lipa and Callum Turner are giving me FOMO like I’ve never had before. After (not so) quietly tying the knot at London’s Old Marylebone Town Hall on May 31, the couple arrived in Palermo for a three-day wedding celebration that reportedly transformed parts of the Sicilian capital into a full luxury lockdown. Equal parts Dolce Vita fantasy and fashion week off-site, the weekend kicked off with welcome drinks at the historic Palazzo Valguarnera Gangi—the same palazzo featured in Luchino Visconti’s The Leopard.
And naturally, the styling understood the assignment. Dua wore a Bottega Veneta halter gown with an open leather basketweave back and ostrich-feather train, finished with gold Bulgari jewelry. Mr. Dua Lipa matched the energy in relaxed Jacquemus tailoring that felt intentionally understated against all the spectacle.
#DuaLipa #CallumTurner #Palermo #BottegaVeneta #Jacquemus

posts.caption:
we went inside Sandro’s Paris atelier to experience something most people never hear.
everyone talks about the finished look. the runway moment. the campaign. the final product.
but before any of that, there’s the sound of a collection being made.
inside the atelier, around 30 people work across sketching, pattern-making, cutting and construction to bring each prototype to life. scissors cutting through fabric, pencils moving across paper, sewing machines running at full speed, small sounds that eventually become a collection.
#TFO #Sandro #SandroParis #Fashion #Atelier

posts.caption:
Christian Lacroix was one of the defining forces of 90s couture
during its rise, the brand became part of LVMH, aligning with the luxury boom of the era. but as fashion shifted in the late 90s and early 2000s toward minimalism and more commercially scalable aesthetics, the maison struggled to maintain the same momentum. rising production costs and a changing market made its couture model increasingly difficult to sustain, and LVMH eventually stepped away in the mid-2000s.
the couture line officially ended in 2009, marking the close of a chapter that had defined one of fashion’s most expressive visual languages. but the archive never disappeared …  it stayed alive in references, runways, and moodboards that kept 90s maximalism waiting for its return
#TFO #couture #christianlacroix

posts.caption:
Rabanne’s party ’26 and spring ’27 collections prove that great style is less about what you wear and more about how you wear it.
Julien Dossena mixes utility with luxury, tailoring with skater shorts, faux fur with precision cuts, and party dressing with an effortless cool that never tries too hard. flea market treasures meeting modernist essentials, ‘90s Italian suiting reworked for now, and the kind of styling that makes you want to raid someone else’s wardrobe.
a collection built on contradictions: city grit and sophistication, masculine and feminine, glamour and restraint.
because sometimes the coolest thing in your wardrobe is attitude.
#TFO #rabanne #juliendossena #fashion #pacorabbane

posts.caption:
Fatherhood is a new role for the actor who first got noticed for his performance in ‘Masaan’ (2015). In fact, it seems this off-camera Vicky Kaushal (@vickykaushal09) is perhaps closer to Deepak Chaudhary, the character he played in the film. A sensitive, ambitious young man coming to terms with the world around him. That character, Kaushal says, changed him. But not for the obvious reasons.
“Until then success, to me, was where you reached [in life]. It was never about where you started from,” he explains. “That character, starting from the crematorium ghat in Benaras and going on to work as an engineer in the Railways in Allahabad [now Prayagraj]... He had a far bigger life arc than I’ve covered in my life. Till today,” he says, tapping the arm rest for emphasis. “After that I started having so much empathy for those around me, especially those from far less privileged backgrounds, because I don’t know where they have started from.”
@travellerprasad in conversation with the Bollywood actor at the link in bio.

posts.caption:
Thirty minutes after this interview was supposed to begin, Vicky Kaushal’s (@vickykaushal09) tall, statuesque frame bursts in through the door at the Matrix office in Khar, Mumbai. “So sorry, bro,” he says, shaking this reporter’s hand with both of his. His day has been busy—his son’s visa was to be sorted, he couldn’t leave his wife alone, and there’s repair work going on in his building, he says, sounding like your average young husband and father with his daily household chores to take care of, not some Bollywood star with a platoon of staff to handle his every whim.
Since becoming a father, Kaushal says he’s come to understand and appreciate “a lot more” of the work his parents did in raising him. “It occurs to me at the most random moments that that [instilling the right values] is now my responsibility towards my son,” he says. “He is going to learn by observing how his parents are thinking, talking, and behaving. So, I’ve realised whatever you want to see reflected in him has to come in your actions rather than words.”
@travellerprasad in conversation with the Bollywood actor at the link in bio.

posts.caption:
“Somewhere around the second trimester, I started a Notes app document called “US list”. It began with three items. It currently has 26. The premise: things that either don’t exist in India, cost three times as much here, or technically ship here but arrive sometime after the baby has started solid foods.
A significant portion of what I know about new motherhood comes from people who’ve already been through it—friends who had their babies a year or two before me, older cousins who want to debrief, mum groups where strangers offer advice with the confidence of someone who has earned it. There’s a lot of noise in all of that, but there’s also genuinely useful signals. And one category of signal that keeps coming up, across all of these sources, is: things you should really try to get from the US.
Some of it will make things easier. Some of it will just look nice on your changing table. I’ve made peace with both.”
At the link in bio @avantidalal on the maternity shopping list she sent her US-based cousin, that you might want to send yours.
@get_nanit @tubbytodd

posts.caption:
“By my bedside is a pigeon with a private message tied to its orange claws, a miniature model handcrafted by Juhu Beach Studio (@juhubeachstudio). When it was gifted to me on my birthday this year, I was thrilled; only someone who really understood me would know how much I’ve come to adore these common city birds that we’ve been taught to revile since childhood. That you shouldn’t just ignore them but shoo them. That they’re not just plain and unremarkable (have you even seen those iridescent flecks of green and purple?) but far worse—foul pests, “rats with wings”.
Unfortunately for any pigeon haters, today you will find just as many pigeons on social media as you do offline in whatever city you call home.”
At the link in bio, @saachisassified on why the Pigeon has become Gen Z’s mascot.

posts.caption:
Rang raja, poth praja, bhanth daasi: colour is king, fabric comes second, and motif serves them both. It’s a saying designer and revivalist Sourav Das (@labelsouravdas) first heard in Varanasi while working with Benarasi weaves and one that quietly became the cornerstone of his design philosophy. “That has been my guiding thought all along,” he says, twisting his long tresses into a bun to combat a particularly unforgiving Hyderabad afternoon. “My work is inspired by everything Indian: colour, material, and technique. The richness of our craft sets the direction.”
Indeed, so striking is everything that passes through his fingers that at a recent exhibition in Hyderabad, guests wanted to buy even the Gangaur dolls he dressed up as decor. And at a showcase at Ensemble in Mumbai earlier this year, the famously reticent Jaya Bachchan was spotted browsing, smiling and even overheard asking daughter Shweta Bachchan Nanda to come and check out the collection.
At the link in bio, Malik Thatipalli takes us inside the multiverse of the Hyderabad-based designer, miniature painter, singer, and dancer.

posts.caption:
Yesterday, an article in the Indian Express on why “clothing and not air conditioning should be the first layer of response to extreme heat”, made a good point about dressing and heat waves.
As temperatures soar, the thing that stands out is how a majority of Indians dress. From domestic helps to daily wage workers in jewellery or garment industries in Surat, Tiruppur or Noida, catering staff, delivery people, hospitality teams or doormen outside five-star hotels, propped as the last vestiges of colonial decorum—isn't there a heatwave in our wardrobes? Most hazardously, even in school uniforms as most are made from synthetic blends. No studies so far confirm the use of airism fabrics in affordable clothing, which adapt to humidity, heat and discomfort. Synthetic saris with machine-washable zari are worn by women from Agra to Ahmedabad. Heavy fabrics are used to uniform security guards. Staff in malls and restaurants wear clothes designed for branding more than breathing. Black, with its intense opacity, has emerged as a standard uniform colour in service and retail industries in India, despite rising temperatures.
India is the second largest producer of polyester and viscose and consumes 6 million metric tonnes of polyester annually (not all goes into clothing). The country has accelerated polyester production because cotton fibre is twice as expensive. Global trends correspond to this change.
For centuries, clothing evolved in response to climate and geography. India’s mulmul, khadi, cotton and other breathable summer fabrics are now only affordable for the elite. The changing economics of fibres, the positioning of handwoven textiles as luxury, the way cotton guzzles water, thus becoming a climate depleting fabric, have led to inequity between the weather and what we wear.
World Environment Day could urge a new question. Not just what we recycle or upcycle but whether our clothes are fit for the climate we now inhabit. In a country facing longer summers and frequent heatwaves, climate-responsive clothing is a public health issue and thus a design imperative. Perhaps a contest at a fashion week or a design festival can put out “Heat as a Design Brief”.

posts.caption:
Hi, I’m Veronica 👋🏼 a sewing pattern designer based in Melbourne, Australia.
I create patterns, tools and resources that help you sew with understanding.
My work focuses on how fabrics behave, how garments are constructed, and how sewing connects us to the history of what we wear.
Sewing Patterns
My patterns are designed for versatility, wearability and fun. They include the Hazel, Freya, Odette, Audrey, Hera, Cordelia and the Sleeve Studio — each combining comfort with thoughtful design details. You’ll also find The Noelle, a free beginner-friendly pattern that introduces my approach to fit and construction.
The Paper Doll
A print-at-home paper doll designed to help you visualise and play with my sewing patterns. You can cut it out and colour it yourself, or use the pre-filled versions featuring my fabric prints and colour palettes. The set includes curvier model options and three skin tones to choose from.
The Conscious Consumer eBook
A practical guide to fabrics, sustainability and sewing with intention. It helps you understand fibre origins, weave structures and how to make long-lasting choices when you sew or buy clothes.
Digital Sewing Tools
Two paid calculators designed for precision:
• Shirring Calculator – works out fabric shrinkage and elastic thread length.
• GSM Calculator – estimates fabric weight and drape.
Available individually or as a discounted bundle.
My PDF sewing patterns are stocked digitally through @fabricgodmother , @spotlightstores , @thefoldline , @joans.finefabric and @thedrapersdaughter .
Fabric and Accessories
You can shop my fabric designs on @spoonflower and find my illustrated phone cases at @thedairy .
Everything I create is designed to help you sew with confidence, understanding and creativity.
For all enquires email us at hello@veronicatuckerthelabel.com

The algorithm thinks we want to be a TradWife but it’s totally misreading us 👀
I’ve spent all week writing about why millennial women are pausing on sourdough content, what the algorithm does with that pause, and what we actually want which is not what we’re being sold.
This essay covers haptic data, religious pipelines, the GirlBoss era’s hangover, gentrification in human form, and why I went from TradWife curious to something else entirely.
Full essay linked in bio. Free to read.
Now tell me, when you see TradWife content, what’s your gut reaction?

posts.caption:
The books that are building The Goddess Project.
Six months of research into the history of draped cloth as a political act and these are the books sitting on my desk, on my @kobobooks Libra Colour, and in my @thequirkycupcollective reading journal while I do it.
From Elizabeth Wayland Barber reconstructing what women were actually doing in the ancient world, to Victoria Bateman exposing the inaccuracies in how that history has been recorded, to Emily Wilson (the first woman to ever translate the Odyssey into English) and Stephen Fry making Homer genuinely funny.
Every one of these books is informing a carousel, an essay, or a podcast episode in the Goddess Project series. If you want to read alongside me, this is the list.
The Goddess Project carousels are live on this page. The Substack essays go deeper 👉🏼 link in bio.
Watch the full video breakdown of this list and more books on my YouTube channel.
Paid partnership with @thequirkycupcollective 🫶🏼 Gifted by @kobobooks_au #ad
posts.alt_text: Photo by Veronica Tucker the Label on October 23, 2025.

posts.caption:
This is so important for the history of human kind, especially women ❤️
Don’t forget the Gaulish as well. They’re often harder to find than Irish or Welsh mythology because much of it was not written down by the Gauls themselves. When the Romans conquered Gaul, a lot of the oral traditions were lost or blended with Roman religion. It survived mostly through inscriptions, archaeology, and Roman accounts rather than through medieval mythological texts like those preserved in Ireland.
Epona, perhaps the most famous Gaulish goddess. Rosmerta, Nantosuelta, Sirona, Sequena (who I love, goddess of the river La Seine in Paris) reflecting a common Celtic belief that rivers, springs, lakes and landscapes possessed divine feminine spirits.
The Matres or Matronae,they are some of the most fascinating feminine figures in continental Celtic religion. Rather than a single goddess, they seem to embody the sacred feminine as a collective force.
This “triple goddess” motif later appears in many Celtic traditions.❤️❤️❤️
posts.alt_text: Photo by Veronica Tucker the Label on October 23, 2025.

posts.caption: Lovely sleeve. I read left to right as is our custom, lol. I prefer the left. It is elevated, whereas the right is flippant and doesn't honor your work - it's more like saying look at my clever funny impression of this work. Also, putting randomly oriented emojis near your photo is not good, because they are cartoonish and draw the eyes away from your beautiful photo. The only thing I didn't like on the left is the description of the shirring as starting at the elbow when it really starts about mid-bicep. I think sewists want the most accurate description. Beautiful work - the dress is wonderful!
posts.alt_text: Photo by Veronica Tucker the Label on October 23, 2025.

posts.caption:
“I don’t want to understand everyone; I want a world where I live and let others live. In New York City, I was staying in the middle of a huge building, surrounded by people of different races and religions, and there was no tension whatsoever. Sloterdijk even talks about how we need to learn a certain kind of discretion. That’s what bothers me — the idea that I have to understand your world. How am I supposed to understand your world when neither you understand it nor I understand my own? I’m very superficial in that regard. You have a neighbor — establish some kind of order, with basic respect, and don’t push yourself onto them, nor let them push themselves onto you. I take great pleasure in discretion. Maybe that comes with age.”
For the May Issue of Vogue Adria, we spoke with @SlavojZizeks, one of today’s most renowned philosophers, about the future of the left, the dangers facing modern society, one night stands, and revolution.

posts.alt_text:
Is Brandy Melville closing its fitting rooms for good? The teen favorite sparked controversy last week when customers began posting about seemingly shuttered in-store fitting rooms online. The brand joins a growing list of stores that have recently closed their fitting rooms. What does this shift mean for customers who can no longer try before they buy?

On the ground in London, Brandy Melville customers who spoke to Vogue Business largely disagreed with the closures, with some consumers making purchases with mixed feelings and others planning to shop elsewhere. For these handful of shoppers, secondhand platform Vinted and fellow high street retailers such as Subdued, Zara and Urban Outfitters came out on top when asked where they would reallocate their spend.

The company has not confirmed the closures and did not respond to requests for comment from Vogue Business.

Tap the link in bio to read more on why some retailers are abandoning the fitting room.

Video: @_aureliarachel

posts.caption:
Chaos is something @GracieAbrams has grown accustomed to. Eight years ago, she was a first-year student at Barnard with a cult fandom she’d fostered through Instagram; soon she was signed to Interscope, then on her first tour, then opening for Olivia Rodrigo and Taylor Swift. By the end of 2024, Abrams was a Grammy-nominated pop star filling arenas.
She finally took a breather last summer. “It’s not until you stop that you realize ‘Oh, I haven’t seen my friends in a long time. I haven’t cooked a meal in a long time,’” Abrams says. “The sleep schedule becomes regular for the first time in, like, four years.”
Now 26, she’s shifting into a new gear. Abrams’s third studio album, “Daughter from Hell,” out in July, sounds like her—earnest, introspective, folk-informed—but the production is more lush (“It feels like me in progress,” she says); she’s also in a happy relationship and making her first film, “Please,” with the filmmaker Halina Reijn.
For Vogue’s Summer 2026 issue, Brittany Spanos meets Gracie Abrams in Los Angeles and New York to talk about growing up, slowing down, falling in love, and where she’d like to go next. Tap the link in our bio to read the full profile.
Photographed by @larissahofmann, Written by @brittanyspanos, Styled by @jordenbickham, Hair by @tamastuzes, Makeup by @emikaneko, Manicure by @1.800.nailme, Tailoring by Irina Tshartaryan for Susie’s Custom Designs, Produced by @hyperion.la, Set Design by  @patienceharding. posts.caption: We've all had those days where you simply cannot fathom the idea of changing out of your pajamas to leave the house. Out and about in Venice, Emma Watson did just that—with a breakfast plate and a glass of OJ at the ready. Tap the link in our bio to discover how Watson knows there's nothing snoozy about pajama dressing, especially when you're sporting sustainable fashions.

posts.caption: Teyana! Keke! Ayo! Now in its 19th year, last night, the stars aligned under one roof for @chanelofficial’s Tribeca Festival Artists Dinner at Robert DeNiro's Tribeca Grill. As co-founder of the film festival, DeNiro raised a glass to commemorate 25 years of Tribeca, which was founded in the wake of devastation post 9/11 in a bid to rebuild the neighborhood. And to top off this quintessentially New York night? Large screens that beamed the action live from MSG right into the room, as attendees got more and more into the buzz. Tap the link in our bio for a look inside the festivities. Photos: @poupayphoto

posts.caption: For our Summer 2026 cover starring @GracieAbrams, we stepped inside the @LucasMuseum of Narrative Art. Showcasing the personal collection of George Lucas and Mellody Hobson, the museum brings together illustrations, artworks, and iconic film artifacts in a tribute to narrative art. Tap the link in our bio to take a closer look inside.

posts.caption:
For @GracieAbrams, the next thing is acting. She met filmmaker Halina Reijn three years ago over breakfast, when Reijn was looking for songwriters to contribute to her 2024 film, “Babygirl.” And when Reijn began writing her follow-up, the script for a film called “Please,” Abrams was “immediately” on her mind—this time to play the lead. Plot details are under wraps (the studio A24 will only tell me it’s “sexy and romantic”), but Reijn says it “provokes something all women can relate to,” and she was impressed with how Abrams connected in an audition.
For Vogue’s Summer 2026 issue, Brittany Spanos meets Gracie Abrams in Los Angeles and New York to talk about growing up, slowing down, falling in love, and where she’d like to go next.
Photographed by @larissahofmann, Written by @brittanyspanos, Styled by @jordenbickham, Hair by @tamastuzes, Makeup by @emikaneko, Manicure by @1.800.nailme, Tailoring by Irina Tshartaryan for Susie’s Custom Designs, Produced by @hyperion.la, Set Design by  @patienceharding. Vogue, Summer 2026.

posts.caption:
Elfie Semotan was 51 when she first walked in a fashion show in the early ’90s for the modernist designer Helmut Lang. For Lang, Semotan’s age was no doubt a benefit, as was the fact that she was not a model, but a photographer who hailed from Austria, as he did. In the era of the impossibly flawless, over-the-top glamazons, Lang went with mannequins who were stripped-back, elegant, and very real.
Semotan died yesterday at the age of 84, in her native Austria. She leaves behind a legacy as an accomplished photographer who specialized in portraiture, landscapes, and still lifes. In memory of her passing, we’ve collected some of her appearances on the designer’s catwalks. Tap the link in bio to see more.
posts.alt_text: Photo by Vogue Runway on June 09, 2026. May be an image of overcoat, kilt, turtleneck and text.

posts.caption: Welcome back to sunny Stockholm, where the summer is short and fashion week is even shorter. In just two days, the Swedish capital crams in a week's worth of shows, events, and excellent style. We're on the ground, keeping tabs on the best street style outside of this season's shows. Tap the link in bio to see more. Photographed by @styledumonde
posts.alt_text: Photo by Vogue Runway on June 09, 2026. May be an image of one or more people and text that says 'VOGUE RUNWAY RUN WAY A LOOK BACK AT ELFIE SEMOTAN, THE HELMUT LANG MUSE WHO HAS DIED AT 84'.

posts.caption:
Chaos is something @GracieAbrams has grown accustomed to. Eight years ago, she was a first-year student at Barnard with a cult fandom she’d fostered through Instagram; soon she was signed to Interscope, then on her first tour, then opening for Olivia Rodrigo and Taylor Swift. By the end of 2024, Abrams was a Grammy-nominated pop star filling arenas.
She finally took a breather last summer. “It’s not until you stop that you realize ‘Oh, I haven’t seen my friends in a long time. I haven’t cooked a meal in a long time,’” Abrams says. “The sleep schedule becomes regular for the first time in, like, four years.”
Now 26, she’s shifting into a new gear. Abrams’s third studio album, “Daughter from Hell,” out in July, sounds like her—earnest, introspective, folk-informed—but the production is more lush (“It feels like me in progress,” she says); she’s also in a happy relationship and making her first film, “Please,” with the filmmaker Halina Reijn.
For Vogue’s Summer 2026 issue, Brittany Spanos meets Gracie Abrams in Los Angeles and New York to talk about growing up, slowing down, falling in love, and where she’d like to go next. Tap the link in our bio to read the full profile.
Photographed by @larissahofmann, Written by @brittanyspanos, Styled by @jordenbickham, Hair by @tamastuzes, Makeup by @emikaneko, Manicure by @1.800.nailme, Tailoring by Irina Tshartaryan for Susie's Custom Designs, Produced by @hyperion.la, Set Design by  @patienceharding. Vogue, Summer 2026.

posts.caption: @Carven has appointed Kai Nesselrath as design director, succeeding Mark Thomas, who exited the French fashion house in April. Nesselrath will present his debut runway collection during #ParisFashionWeek SS27 in October. "A new generation’s perspective on the world feels especially important today,” Shawna Tao, CEO of @Carven, which is part of China’s ICCF Group, said in a statement. “The essence of Carven is a fresh and courageous creator’s spirit, and we believe Kai is uniquely suited to interpret and express it.” @LaureGuilbault shares more details at the link in bio.
posts.alt_text:
Introducing our latest series the ‘Future of AI’. This week, at #VogueBusiness, we’re looking at what comes next for AI in the context of fashion, beauty and luxury, with a collection of 10 articles and corresponding images — all created using human intelligence. As part of the series, @ElektraKotsoni asked ChatGPT about #AI’s role in the future. Its response? Not bad, but we could have said it better — so we did. Tap the link in bio to discover the articles.

Photographer @shahramsaadat
Art director @itsniall_
Creative content producer @_aureliarachel

posts.caption: Piecing together an outfit comes naturally to @Maria_McManus_. “I love two or three-piece dressing because I can put more ideas together," says the Irish-born designer known for her sustainable approach to wardrobing. McManus, who calls New York City home but travels extensively, has come to appreciate the structure of a uniform and a refined color palette. Splitting her time between Manhattan, Montauk, and Ireland, McManus shares with Vogue how she does summer-in-the-city style in a week’s worth of outfits. See her breakdown and shop her looks at the link in bio.

posts.caption:
✨ Creator, Actor, PHENOMENON! ✨ On the June-July cover of Wedding Vows, we present the trailblazing @mostlysane, Prajakta Koli!
From building a YouTube empire to being the lead for Netflix Original Mismatched, from being part of Michelle Obama’s Day Time Emmy winning docu-series to being UNDP India's First Youth Climate Champion, her journey is nothing short of extraordinary.
With her debut romance novel set to launch this year, along with her first Amazon Prime Original Andhera, Prajakta’s journey is a testament to the power of dreams, hard work, and staying true to oneself. She continues to inspire millions with her authenticity, relatability, and unstoppable spirit.

posts.caption:
@camimendes is having one of the biggest years in her career so far. A major summer blockbuster, the second feature film from her production company, and wedding planning are all on the roster for 2026. This month, Mendes steps into the sprawling world of Masters of the Universe as the sword-slinging Teela alongside Nicholas Galitzine, Idris Elba, and Jared Leto.
The project is a stretch from her days as Veronica Lodge in #Riverdale, which catapulted her into mainstream recognition nearly a decade ago. Mendes makes movies for “the girls and the gays,” she states lovingly, but her star power is only going to get bigger and brighter with Masters. “I’ve never been a part of something of this scale,” she says of the project.
In our Now Playing Issue, presented by @bvlgari, we chat with the actress about reinventing herself in this era of her life. Read it all in the full interview at the link in our bio.

posts.caption:
There would be no Pope Heyward without @jonathandavissofficial. Much like his smart, careful, deeply moral Outer Banks character, Daviss knows what it feels like to carry the pressure of proving your worth and refusing to let yourself or anyone else down. “What really stood out to me about Pope in season 1 was this idea of expectations and pressure that gets put on a lot of young Black teens and teens in general,” he says. “There’s this pressure to succeed, and it was something I truly have been through.”
Daviss still thinks back to his early audition days before landing the life-changing role on OBX, when the constant need to prove himself as a young actor felt emotionally exhausting. His mindset then? “You only lose when you quit.”
Head to the link in our bio for the full download in our Now Playing Issue presented by @bvlgari, from what to expect from the fifth and final season of OBX to his upcoming portrayal of a young Snoop Dogg.

posts.caption:
@victoriamonet is trading her claws for butterfly wings. The three-time Grammy winner is the definition of a multi-hyphenate: a culinary school graduate, a mother, and, of course, a singer-songwriter with a nearly two-decade-spanning catalog to her name. Now, using her 37th birthday as a launching pad, Monét has cleared her mental space and scrubbed her socials to prepare for her next chapter—an album she’s dubbed her very own Thriller, a sonically surprising, genre-defying project that marks a softer evolution. “The jaguar exists within me always, but I’m feeling like a softer version of myself now,” she says.
After all, Monét’s critically acclaimed debut studio album Jaguar II was the ultimate learning lesson. “Once you have an accolade like that, people often look for a reason to discredit why you got it,” she explains. “I think—pending a Grammy happens again—I would do the opposite and bunker down in the studio.”
Step inside Monét’s world in our Now Playing Issue, presented by @bvlgari, at the link in our bio.

posts.caption:
@jodiemcomer has never played it safe. ⁠
⁠
After captivating audiences as Villanelle in "Killing Eve," the actress is stepping into quieter, more complex territory with "The Death of Robin Hood." For WWD Weekend's latest cover story, Comer opens up about exploring darker creative spaces, embracing the projects that scare her most and why pushing boundaries still drives her. ⁠
⁠
Read the interview now at the link in bio.⁠
⁠

posts.caption:
“Knicks in four!” cheered Teyana Taylor as she made her way down the red carpet. The actor’s time at the Chanel Artists Dinner would be brief: she was in fact headed to the game, and after making the rounds at the party would be doing an outfit change in a sprinter van en route to Madison Square Garden.
As with the rest of New York City currently, the Knicks were all anyone could talk about on Monday at the annual event, now in its 19th year. Tribeca Festival’s Robert De Niro and Jane Rosenthal welcomed a group to Tribeca Grill, owned by De Niro, which closed in 2025 and will be revived by Major Food Group in 2027. Monday’s dinner was the first event to be held in the new Tribeca Grill iteration.
Read more at WWD.com — #linkinbio
Report/Videos: @leighnordstrom
Photos: @lexieblacklock

posts.caption:
Chanel has appointed Marie-Laure Cérède as director of its jewelry creation studio, the French house announced Tuesday.⁠
⁠
Set to join the company in October, she will be in charge of all precious jewelry and high jewelry creations and will report to Frédéric Grangié, president of Chanel watches and fine jewelry. She succeeds Patrice Leguéreau, who held the position for 15 years until his death in November 2024.⁠
⁠
Based in the French house’s 18 Place Vendôme building, the new jewelry head will work closely with teams in Paris and Geneva.⁠
⁠
Read more at WWD.com — #linkinbio⁠
⁠
Report: Lily Templeton

posts.caption:
MAC Cosmetics has revealed its latest Viva Glam face.⁠
⁠
Chappell Roan, fronting an Inez & Vinoodh-lensed campaign, will join the ranks of RuPaul, Lady Gaga, Rihanna, Kim Petras and more with a trio of Viva Glam products including a matte lipstick in shade "Unnatural Red Head," a sheer lipstick in "Roan of Arc" and a gloss in "Damnsel."⁠
⁠
"Viva Glam has always been about more than makeup. It's about showing up for communities and making real change," Roan said in a statement. ⁠
⁠
Read more on WWD.com — #linkinbio⁠
⁠

posts.caption:
With the queen of pop in one hand and an inaugural U.S. retail partnership in the other, Kiko Milano is ready to take on the American makeup market.
The Italian beauty brand, which in 2025 crossed the $1 billion sales threshold for the first time, is marking its first big U.S. push with a Macy’s launch and a partnership with Madonna, who joins the 29-year-old brand as its second-ever global ambassador.
“We collectively decided that we wanted to come to the U.S. and make a scene,” said Drew Elliott, who joined Kiko as chief brand officer in 2025, exiting his previous role as global creative director at MAC Cosmetics.
Read more on WWD.com — #linkinbio
Report: @noorlobaddie
📸: @kikomilano

posts.caption:
When @rockyhorrorbway director Sam Pinkleton called and asked @stephaniehsuofficial if she wanted to be his Janet, she had a confession: she was a virgin.⁠
⁠
“I was like, ’I’ve actually never seen Rocky Horror,’” says the actress, wearing a T-shirt bearing Pinkleton’s likeness and seated on the mezzanine level of the Roundabout Theater at Studio 54 before a recent matinee performance. “So I watched the movie that night, and fell in love with it, and ever since have been deep diving into the fandom,” Hsu adds. “I’ve gone to the midnight showings, watched the documentary, and now I’m definitely obsessed.“⁠
⁠
The actress stars in the Broadway revival as Janet Weiss, a coquettish woman who, along with her husband Brad, stumbles upon Dr. Frank-N-Furter’s mansion one fateful night. Inside, an eclectic cast of characters and sexual awakening awaits them. The show received nine Tony nominations ahead of this weekend’s awards, and Hsu is nominated for best leading actress in a musical for her portrayal.⁠
⁠
Read more at WWD.com — #linkinbio⁠
⁠
Report: @ktauer⁠
Photos: @rywilliams.co/WWD
